idea.hook: Think our galaxy is big? You haven't seen the Universe's true giants!
facts: Our Milky Way galaxy stretches 100,000 light-years across, home to hundreds of billions of stars.
But galaxies are just tiny beads on the Cosmic Web, a vast, foam-like network of filaments and voids.
The Hercules-Corona Borealis Great Wall, the largest known structure, spans over 10 billion light-years.
idea.payoff: That's roughly 1/10th the size of the *entire observable universe!* Imagine traversing that.
idea.title: Universe's LARGEST Structure! 🤯🌌

scenes.visual: A vibrant, rotating spiral galaxy (like the Milky Way) against a backdrop of distant stars.

scenes.text: Milky Way: 100,000 light-years across. Billions of stars.
scenes.visual: Zooming out from the Milky Way, showing its full scale, with text overlay. Stars subtly twinkle.

scenes.text: Galaxies are tiny beads on the Cosmic Web.
scenes.visual: Transition to an abstract, interconnected visualization of the Cosmic Web: glowing filaments, dark voids, and galaxies as bright nodes.
Zoom out dramatically from the Cosmic Web, highlighting an immense, luminous filament structure, representing the Hercules-Corona Borealis Great Wall.
A breathtaking visualization of the entire observable universe, with the Great Wall subtly highlighted as a vast, but proportionally smaller, structure.
